Musical artist

**Keshia Chanté Harper** (born June 16, 1988) is a Canadian [singer-songwriter](/wiki/Singer-songwriter "Singer-songwriter"), [television host](/wiki/Television_presenter "Television presenter"), [actress](/wiki/Actress "Actress") and entrepreneur.

She has served as a judge on the international music competition series [*The World's Best*](/wiki/The_World%27s_Best "The World's Best"),[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) hosted *The Legacy Awards*,[[3]](#cite_note-3) been an on-air reporter and host for *[Entertainment Tonight Canada](/wiki/Entertainment_Tonight_Canada "Entertainment Tonight Canada")*,[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-:1-5) and is listed as an International Voter for the [Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Chanté has appeared on, or hosted, several television programs including the [BET Awards](/wiki/BET_Awards "BET Awards"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) Paramount’s *Peak of the Week*,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) hosted *BB Rewind,* the official after show of [season 22](/wiki/Big_Brother_22_(American_season) "Big Brother 22 (American season)") of *[Big Brother](/wiki/Big_Brother_(American_TV_series) "Big Brother (American TV series)")* on [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS"),[[8]](#cite_note-MonstersBB-8) and *[Battle of the Blades](/wiki/Battle_of_the_Blades "Battle of the Blades")*.[[9]](#cite_note-9) She has also participated in community and charitable initiatives, including hosting [We Day](/wiki/We_Day "We Day") and supporting campaigns focused on mental health awareness.[[10]](#cite_note-:2-10)[[11]](#cite_note-:3-11) In 2022, she founded the hair care brand KHAIR.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In 2014, she received a star on the [Brampton Arts Walk of Fame](/wiki/Brampton_Arts_Walk_of_Fame "Brampton Arts Walk of Fame") for her contributions to Canadian entertainment.[[13]](#cite_note-BramptonCity-13)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Keshia Chanté Harper was born and raised in [Ottawa](/wiki/Ottawa "Ottawa"), [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario"), and spent summers with her grandparents in [Hinesville, Georgia](/wiki/Hinesville,_Georgia "Hinesville, Georgia"). Her father is of [Afro-Trinidadian](/wiki/Afro%E2%80%93Trinidadians_and_Tobagonians "Afro–Trinidadians and Tobagonians") descent and her mother is of [Puerto Rican](/wiki/Puerto_Ricans "Puerto Ricans") and [Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_people "Portuguese people") descent.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

She moved to the Greater Toronto Area as a teenager and graduated from [Fletcher's Meadow Secondary School](/wiki/Fletcher%27s_Meadow_Secondary_School "Fletcher's Meadow Secondary School") in 2006.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### 2003–2012

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2003–2012")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e3/Keshia_Chant%C3%A9_%282010%29_-_2.jpg/250px-Keshia_Chant%C3%A9_%282010%29_-_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Keshia_Chant%C3%A9_(2010)_-_2.jpg)

Keshia Chanté (2010)

In 2003, Chanté released the singles "Shook (The Answer)," and "Unpredictable",[[16]](#cite_note-16) in response to [Shawn Desman](/wiki/Shawn_Desman "Shawn Desman")’s "Shook".[[17]](#cite_note-Now2004-17) The video for her single "Bad Boy"[[18]](#cite_note-18) was directed by [Director X](/wiki/Director_X "Director X").[[5]](#cite_note-:1-5)

Her self‑titled debut album was released in 2004.[[17]](#cite_note-Now2004-17) In October 2004, at the [Canadian Urban Music Awards](/wiki/Urban_Music_Association_of_Canada "Urban Music Association of Canada"), Chanté won Best New Artist, Video of the Year (for "Bad Boy"), and the Fan’s Choice Award.[[19]](#cite_note-CTV2004-19) That month she also performed the national anthem at the [CFL](/wiki/Canadian_Football_League "Canadian Football League")’s [Grey Cup](/wiki/Grey_Cup "Grey Cup") championship game.[[20]](#cite_note-OttawaStart2011-20)

Her second album, *2U*, was released by [Sony Music Canada](/wiki/Sony_Music "Sony Music") in 2006, in Canada and Japan. That same year, her MTV special "*The Diary of Keshia Chanté*" aired in December.[[21]](#cite_note-Canoe2006-21)[[22]](#cite_note-ElleCanada-22)

Her third album, *Night & Day*, was released in 2011 by [Universal Music Canada](/wiki/List_of_Universal_Music_Group_labels "List of Universal Music Group labels").[[23]](#cite_note-23) Singles "Test Drive" and "Table Dancer" received [Juno Award](/wiki/Juno_Awards "Juno Awards") nominations (R&B/Soul Recording of the Year and Dance Recording of the Year, respectively).[[24]](#cite_note-Juno2011-24) She performed on ET Canada’s New Year’s Eve show in [Niagara Falls](/wiki/Niagara_Falls,_Ontario "Niagara Falls, Ontario").[[25]](#cite_note-ShawNYE-25) In late 2012, "Table Dancer" reached the [Billboard Japan](/wiki/Billboard_Japan "Billboard Japan") charts, including Hot 100 and Digital & Airplay.[[26]](#cite_note-BillboardAirplay-26)[[27]](#cite_note-BillboardDA-27)

### 2013–2015

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2013–2015")]

Chanté co‑hosted *[106 & Park](/wiki/106_%26_Park "106 & Park")* on [BET](/wiki/BET "BET") alongside [Bow Wow](/wiki/Bow_Wow_(rapper) "Bow Wow (rapper)") during 2013–2014, and appeared as an on‑air correspondent at BET events.[[28]](#cite_note-BETsite-28) In December 2014, she received a star on the [Brampton Arts Walk of Fame](/wiki/Brampton_Arts_Walk_of_Fame "Brampton Arts Walk of Fame").[[29]](#cite_note-BramptonAwoF-29)[[30]](#cite_note-:4-30)

### 2016–2022

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2016–2022")]

BET premiered Chanté’s song "The Valley" in November 2016.[[31]](#cite_note-31) In March 2017, [Noisey](/wiki/Noisey "Noisey") premiered "Red Light"[[32]](#cite_note-NoiseyRedLight-32) and A.Side (AUX) premiered "Harmless",[[33]](#cite_note-AUXHarmless-33) followed by the release of the EP *Unbound 01* (2017) and *Unbound 02* (2018).[[32]](#cite_note-NoiseyRedLight-32)[[34]](#cite_note-AllMusicValley-34) *Unbound 01* received a Juno nomination in 2018.[[35]](#cite_note-Juno2018-35)

In 2020, Chanté hosted *Battle of the Blades* with Ron MacLean on CBC Television.[[36]](#cite_note-CBCBoB-36) She also hosted *BB Rewind*, the official after show of season 22 of *Big Brother*.[[8]](#cite_note-MonstersBB-8)

### 2023–present

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2023–present")]

In 2022, Chanté launched the hair care brand KHAIR.[[37]](#cite_note-NewsfileKHAIR-37) In April 2023, she was listed as an International Voter for the Golden Globe Awards.[[38]](#cite_note-GoldenGlobesVoters-38) In April 2025, she appeared in the [Lifetime](/wiki/Lifetime_(TV_channel) "Lifetime (TV channel)") film *Fame: A Temptations Story* alongside Keri Hilson.[[39]](#cite_note-RatedRFilm-39)

In June 2025, Chanté participated in the 25th‑anniversary *106 & Park* tribute segment at the BET Awards in Los Angeles.[[40]](#cite_note-40)[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42)

## Musical style

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Musical style")]

Tara Henley of *[The Georgia Straight](/wiki/The_Georgia_Straight "The Georgia Straight")* wrote that Chanté has "an arresting voice, magnetic charisma, and driving ambition."[[43]](#cite_note-43) Denise Sheppard of Amazon.ca described Chanté as "a teen‑targeted pop phenom" whose "music has the one‑two punch of melodic hooks and the voice to back them up; a combination that can and will appeal to dance/pop music fans of any age."[[44]](#cite_note-44)

### Influences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Influences")]

Chanté has cited [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé"), [Aaliyah](/wiki/Aaliyah "Aaliyah"), and [Brandy](/wiki/Brandy_Norwood "Brandy Norwood") among her childhood influences, as well as [Tupac Shakur](/wiki/Tupac_Shakur "Tupac Shakur"), with whom she shares a birthday.[[45]](#cite_note-45)

## Public image

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Public image")]

In Canada, Chanté has been described as a fashionista by national outlets, performing at [Marchesa](/wiki/Marchesa_(brand) "Marchesa (brand)")’s first Toronto runway show and appearing at a [Chanel](/wiki/Chanel "Chanel") event during the [Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/Toronto_International_Film_Festival "Toronto International Film Festival").[[46]](#cite_note-ctv.ca-46)[[30]](#cite_note-:4-30) She later served as a judge on *Canada’s Best Beauty Talent* alongside [Coco Rocha](/wiki/Coco_Rocha "Coco Rocha") and *[Flare](/wiki/Flare_(magazine) "Flare (magazine)")* editor‑in‑chief Lisa Tant.[[47]](#cite_note-47)

### Endorsements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Endorsements")]

Chanté has endorsed brands such as [CANON](/wiki/Canon_Inc. "Canon Inc."), [Pepsi](/wiki/Pepsi "Pepsi"), [Bacardi](/wiki/Bacardi "Bacardi"), [Nestlé](/wiki/Nestl%C3%A9 "Nestlé"), [Bell](/wiki/Bell_Canada "Bell Canada"), [Rogers](/wiki/Rogers_Communications "Rogers Communications"), Stila Cosmetics, [MAC Cosmetics](/wiki/MAC_Cosmetics "MAC Cosmetics"), [Ecko Red](/wiki/Ecko_Records "Ecko Records") and [Sony](/wiki/Sony "Sony").[[30]](#cite_note-:4-30) In 2007, Chanté served as the face of [Ontario Tourism](/wiki/Tourism_in_Ontario "Tourism in Ontario") and sang the theme song in their commercials.[[48]](#cite_note-swaymag.ca-48) In November 2010, Chanté became the face of the [Pepsi Refresh Project](/wiki/Pepsi_Refresh_Project "Pepsi Refresh Project") campaign.[[30]](#cite_note-:4-30)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Chanté divides her time between [West Hollywood, California](/wiki/West_Hollywood,_California "West Hollywood, California"), and [Toronto, Ontario](/wiki/Toronto,_Ontario "Toronto, Ontario").[[49]](#cite_note-49)

## Philanthropy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keshia_Chant%C3%A9&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Philanthropy")]

Chanté has supported HIV/AIDS awareness and youth initiatives in Canada and abroad.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

She has worked with [World Vision](/wiki/World_Vision_International "World Vision International") in the [Dominican Republic](/wiki/Dominican_Republic "Dominican Republic").[[51]](#cite_note-51) She also participated in an AIDS awareness fundraiser with [Alicia Keys](/wiki/Alicia_Keys "Alicia Keys") in support of the [Stephen Lewis Foundation](/wiki/Stephen_Lewis_Foundation "Stephen Lewis Foundation").[[52]](#cite_note-52)[[53]](#cite_note-53)

In 2014, Chanté appeared in an [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") public service announcement for [Mariska Hargitay](/wiki/Mariska_Hargitay "Mariska Hargitay")’s [Joyful Heart Foundation](/wiki/Joyful_Heart_Foundation "Joyful Heart Foundation") alongside [Eli Manning](/wiki/Eli_Manning "Eli Manning") and [Hilary Swank](/wiki/Hilary_Swank "Hilary Swank") to raise awareness of domestic violence.[[30]](#cite_note-:4-30) She has hosted [We Day](/wiki/We_Day "We Day") events in Calgary and Ottawa and spoken about mental health awareness, including depression and anxiety.[[10]](#cite_note-:2-10)[[11]](#cite_note-:3-11)
